title: Gymnanthes polyandra
intro: Gymnanthes polyandra, commonly known as the Polyandrous Gymnanthes, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Gymnanthes polyandra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.

It is classified within the genus Gymnanthes, which is a group of evergreen shrubs. The family Rutaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the citrus family.
